wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the I Am Really Sorry project overview. Explain the project's purpose as an interactive apology creation and delivery platform that transforms the awkward task of apologizing into an engaging, entertaining experience. Document the key features including the interactive apology creation wizard, personalized experiences with recipient names, 3D animated heart visualization, physics-based runaway button, and internationalization support for 11 languages. Describe the target audience, use cases, and the innovative approach to digital communication. Include the technology stack overview, architectural philosophy, and how the platform addresses the challenge of meaningful digital expressions of remorse. Provide context for both casual users and developers interested in understanding the project's scope and capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the audio system that powers the interactive apology experience. Document the sound caching mechanism implemented in useSounds.ts, including audio preload strategies, memory management, and autoplay policy compliance. Explain the sound effect management system for different interactive elements like button clicks, progress meter sounds, and celebration effects. Detail the volume control implementation, audio context handling, and browser autoplay restrictions. Include integration patterns showing how components consume the sound system, error handling for audio loading failures, and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of adding new sound effects, configuring audio volumes, and optimizing audio performance. Address cross-browser compatibility issues, mobile device audio limitations, and best practices for user experience with sound effects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Apology Creation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Apology Creation System that enables users to craft personalized apologies. Document the multi-step wizard interface including scenario selection from predefined categories, recipient identification and customization, language configuration supporting 11 languages, and link generation for sharing. Explain the CreatorFlow component architecture, form validation, state management, and data persistence. Detail the scenario system with different apology contexts, recipient name processing, and language detection mechanisms. Include practical examples of creating various types of apologies, configuration options for customization, and integration patterns with the sharing system. Address user workflows, common use cases, and troubleshooting for form submission issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the I Am Really Sorry platform. Detail the Next.js 13+ App Router implementation with dynamic language routing using catch-all routes. Explain the server-side rendering strategy, static generation for SEO optimization, and client-side hydration patterns. Document the component hierarchy from root layout to individual interactive components. Describe the separation of concerns between layout components, page components, and reusable UI elements. Include the routing architecture for language-specific URLs, canonical URL handling, and SEO metadata generation. Address the build configuration, asset optimization, and deployment pipeline. Explain how the architecture supports progressive enhancement and offline functionality through PWA features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the I Am Really Sorry project. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, package manager options (npm, yarn, pnpm, bun), and development environment setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions starting from repository cloning through dependency installation. Document the development server startup process with multiple package manager options and local server access. Explain the project structure briefly and how to begin customizing the application. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, environment variable configuration, and initial deployment considerations. Make this accessible to developers of all experience levels while ensuring they can quickly get the project running locally.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the animation framework that brings life to the interactive apology experience. Document the Framer Motion integration patterns used throughout the application, including component transitions, entrance/exit animations, and gesture-based interactions. Explain the dramatic text transition system implemented in DramaticText.tsx, covering character-by-character animations, timing controls, and text reveal effects. Detail the floating emoji system in FloatingEmojis.tsx, including particle physics simulation, animation curves, and performance optimization techniques. Document animation state management, concurrent animation handling, and cleanup procedures. Include examples of creating custom animations, configuring animation properties, and integrating animations with user interactions. Address animation performance considerations, browser compatibility, and accessibility guidelines for motion-sensitive users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Experience Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Interactive Experience Engine that orchestrates the personalized apology journey. Document the ApologyExperience component as the central controller managing the complete user interaction flow. Explain the hero section with personalized greetings, remorse quantification system that breaks the 100% barrier, reason enumeration with emoji integration, and promise section with commitment statements. Detail the LandingContent component for initial user engagement, DramaticText component for text transitions and animations, and the overall narrative flow. Include state management patterns, conditional rendering logic, user interaction handling, and dynamic content generation. Provide examples of different experience configurations, customization options, and integration with other interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internationalization System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed internationalization system documentation for the multilingual apology platform. Explain the dynamic dictionary loading mechanism that loads language files on-demand based on URL locale parameters. Document the 11 supported languages including Arabic (RTL) and their respective JSON dictionary structures. Detail the locale detection algorithms, fallback mechanisms, and runtime language switching capabilities. Describe the integration with Next.js dynamic routing for language-specific URLs and canonical URL generation. Include the implementation of right-to-left language support, character encoding considerations, and cultural adaptation strategies. Address the SEO implications of multilingual content, hreflang attributes, and localized metadata generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the I Am Really Sorry platform. Describe the Next.js 13+ App Router architecture with dynamic language routing and server-side rendering capabilities. Document the component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns between presentation, animation, audio, and content layers. Explain the internationalization architecture with dynamic dictionary loading and locale-specific routing. Detail the technology stack including React 19, TypeScript, Framer Motion for animations, Three.js with React Three Fiber for 3D graphics, and Tailwind CSS for styling. Document the data flow patterns from apology creation through interactive experience execution. Include system boundaries, component relationships, and integration patterns. Address performance considerations, lazy loading strategies, and optimization techniques used throughout the architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the styling system that provides the visual foundation for the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration, including custom theme extensions, breakpoint definitions, and utility class usage patterns. Explain the responsive design principles implemented across components, covering mobile-first approach, adaptive layouts, and cross-device compatibility. Detail the color scheme system, typography hierarchy, and spacing conventions used throughout the application. Document the internationalization styling considerations for right-to-left languages, font loading strategies, and CSS-in-JS integration patterns. Include examples of creating custom styles, extending the design system, and maintaining consistency across components. Address performance optimization through CSS optimization, critical path styling, and component-scoped styles. Provide guidelines for theme customization, dark mode implementation, and accessibility compliance in the styling system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Elements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the interactive elements that provide engaging user interactions throughout the apology experience. Document the RunawayButtonI18n component with physics-based movement mechanics, increasing difficulty levels, collision detection, and responsive behavior. Explain the SorryMeterI18n component featuring animated progress visualization, sound feedback integration, and visual indicators. Detail the FloatingEmojis component for particle effects, animation timing, and performance optimization. Include the physics simulation systems, animation frameworks, event handling patterns, and cross-platform compatibility considerations. Provide implementation examples, customization options for behaviors and appearances, and integration guidelines with the main experience flow. Address performance optimization techniques and accessibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation & Graphics Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the animation and graphics architecture layer. Detail the integration of Framer Motion for component transitions, entrance animations, and state-based animations. Explain the Three.js implementation with React Three Fiber for 3D heart animations including physics simulation, lighting models, and material properties. Document the confetti particle system using canvas-confetti for celebratory effects. Describe the sound system architecture with audio caching, autoplay policy compliance, and volume management. Include the CSS animation framework, responsive design patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Address the coordination between 2D animations, 3D graphics, and audio effects to create cohesive interactive experiences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core features of the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document the complete apology creation system including the multi-step wizard, scenario selection, recipient identification, and language configuration. Explain the interactive apology experience engine that orchestrates the personalized journey through hero sections, remorse quantification, reason enumeration, and forgiveness interactions. Detail the physics-based interactive elements including the runaway button mechanics and sorry meter animation. Describe the 3D heart visualization system with realistic physics and visual effects. Include the celebration system, sound integration, and floating emoji effects. Provide usage examples, configuration options, and integration patterns for each feature. Address user workflows, common use cases, and customization possibilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Visual Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the visual systems that create the immersive 3D experience and audio integration. Document the Heart3D component featuring Three.js integration, 3D heart modeling, realistic physics simulation, lighting effects, and material properties. Explain the sound system architecture including audio caching mechanisms, autoplay policy compliance, volume controls, and sound effect management. Detail the CSS animation framework, responsive design patterns, color schemes, and visual styling systems. Include Three.js configuration, WebGL optimization, audio resource management, and performance considerations. Provide examples of customizing visual effects, integrating new animations, and optimizing for different devices. Address browser compatibility, accessibility features, and troubleshooting common visual and audio issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed performance architecture documentation for the interactive apology platform. Explain the Next.js optimization strategies including automatic code splitting, dynamic imports for 3D components, and lazy loading patterns. Document the bundle optimization techniques, asset compression, and CDN integration strategies. Detail the audio resource management with caching mechanisms, preload strategies, and autoplay policy compliance. Describe the 3D graphics optimization including geometry simplification, texture compression, and render performance tuning. Include the TypeScript configuration for strict type checking and build optimization. Address the linting and formatting configurations for code quality. Document monitoring strategies, performance metrics collection, and optimization tracking methodologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technical Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the technical components that power the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document the audio system including sound caching mechanisms, autoplay compliance, volume control, and integration patterns. Explain the animation framework using Framer Motion for component transitions, entrance/exit animations, and performance optimization. Detail the styling system with Tailwind CSS configuration, responsive design principles, color schemes, and typography. Document the confetti celebration system, dramatic text transitions, and floating emoji effects. Include component lifecycle management, state management patterns, and reusable utility functions. Provide implementation details, configuration options, and integration examples for each technical component. Address performance considerations, browser compatibility, and optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internationalization System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the internationalization system powering the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document the dynamic dictionary loading architecture, locale detection mechanisms, and language switching functionality. Explain the 11 supported languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Russian, Portuguese, Turkish, Arabic, Hindi, and Japanese. Detail the RTL language support implementation for Arabic, SEO metadata generation, canonical URL handling, and locale-specific routing. Document the dictionary structure, translation key organization, and content management patterns. Include the integration with the apology creation flow, interactive components, and 3D elements. Provide guidelines for adding new languages, managing translations, and maintaining consistency across the platform. Address cultural considerations, right-to-left layout handling, and accessibility requirements for international users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the component library that forms the foundation of the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document each reusable component including ApologyExperience, CreatorFlow, RunawayButton variants, SorryMeter components, Heart3D visualization, and animation components. For each component, describe the visual appearance, behavior, user interaction patterns, props/attributes, events, and customization options. Include usage examples with code snippets, integration patterns, and composition guidelines. Document component states, animations, transitions, and responsive behavior. Provide accessibility compliance guidelines, cross-browser compatibility considerations, and performance optimization tips. Include component lifecycle management, state management patterns, and integration with the broader system architecture. Address theming support, style customization, and component extension possibilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for performance optimization strategies employed in the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document the lazy loading implementation for 3D components, audio resource management, and bundle splitting techniques. Explain the optimization of Three.js rendering, sound caching mechanisms, and animation performance. Document memory management strategies, component re-rendering optimization, and browser performance considerations. Include profiling techniques, performance monitoring approaches, and optimization best practices. Address mobile performance considerations, progressive enhancement strategies, and graceful degradation patterns. Provide metrics collection, performance budgeting, and continuous optimization workflows. Include troubleshooting guides for common performance bottlenecks and their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for the I Am Really Sorry platform. Document the Next.js build configuration, environment variable management, and deployment preparation. Explain the build optimization process, asset bundling, and production deployment strategies. Detail the CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and release management processes. Document environment configuration for development, staging, and production environments. Include monitoring and analytics setup, error tracking, and performance monitoring. Address security considerations, SSL/TLS configuration, and content security policies. Provide deployment automation scripts, rollback procedures, and maintenance workflows. Include scaling considerations, CDN configuration, and load balancing strategies. Document backup procedures, disaster recovery planning, and operational runbooks for platform maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing guidelines for the I Am Really Sorry project. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# I Am Really Sorry - Interactive Apology Experience

## Project Introduction

**I Am Really Sorry** is a web-based interactive experience designed to help users create and deliver heartfelt apologies through a gamified, multimedia approach. The project transforms the often awkward task of apologizing into an engaging, entertaining experience that combines modern web technologies with emotional storytelling.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as both a practical tool for crafting meaningful apologies and an artistic expression of remorse, utilizing cutting-edge web technologies to create an immersive, emotionally resonant experience.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Emotional Expression**: Provide a creative outlet for expressing genuine remorse
- **User Engagement**: Transform a mundane task into an interactive, entertaining experience
- **Accessibility**: Support multiple languages with culturally appropriate content
- **Technical Innovation**: Demonstrate modern web development practices with 3D graphics and sound

### Target Audience
- Individuals seeking to apologize sincerely to friends, family, or romantic partners
- Users interested in creative digital experiences
- International audiences requiring multilingual support
- Tech enthusiasts exploring modern web technologies

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern Next.js 13+ architecture with App Router, implementing a sophisticated component hierarchy that balances functionality with visual appeal.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Language-Specific Layout]
    B --> C[Main Page Handler]
    C --> D[Creator Flow]
    C --> E[Landing Content]
    C --> F[Apology Experience]
    
    F --> G[Sounds System]
    F --> H[3D Heart Animation]
    F --> I[Interactive Elements]
    
    D --> J[Multi-step Form]
    D --> K[Language Selection]
    D --> L[Link Generation]
    
    I --> M[Runaway Button]
    I --> N[Sorry Meter]
    I --> O[Floating Emojis]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

**Presentation Layer**: React components handling UI logic and user interactions
**Animation Layer**: Framer Motion for smooth transitions and 3D animations  
**Audio Layer**: Custom sound management system with caching
**Content Layer**: Dynamic internationalization with JSON dictionaries
**3D Graphics Layer**: Three.js integration for immersive visual elements

### Design Patterns

- **Component Composition**: Modular React components with clear separation of concerns
- **Internationalization Pattern**: Dynamic dictionary loading based on locale
- **State Management**: Client-side state with React hooks for interactive elements
- **Lazy Loading**: Dynamic imports for 3D components to optimize performance

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Root Layout** (`src/app/layout.tsx`)
- Minimal wrapper providing global structure
- Delegates language-specific rendering to child components

**Language-Specific Layout** (`src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx`)
- Implements static generation for multiple locales
- Handles SEO metadata and Open Graph integration
- Manages RTL/LTR directionality for Arabic content

**Primary Page Handler** (`src/app/[lang]/page.tsx`)
- Orchestrates between creator flow and receiver experience
- Implements conditional rendering based on URL parameters
- Integrates internationalization dictionaries

### Core Modules

**Creator Flow** (`src/components/CreatorFlow.tsx`)
- Multi-step wizard guiding users through apology creation
- Scenario selection with contextual responses
- Language and recipient customization
- Dynamic link generation with copy functionality

**Apology Experience** (`src/components/ApologyExperience.tsx`)
- Central interactive experience for recipients
- Multi-section layout with animated transitions
- Integrated 3D heart visualization
- Sound system coordination

**Interactive Components**
- **Runaway Button** (`src/components/RunawayButtonI18n.tsx`): Physics-based button that moves away from user input
- **Sorry Meter** (`src/components/SorryMeterI18n.tsx`): Animated progress indicator with sound effects
- **3D Heart** (`src/components/Heart3D.tsx`): Real-time beating heart animation using Three.js

### Configuration Approach

**Internationalization System** (`src/app/[lang]/dictionaries.ts`)
- Dynamic import architecture for language files
- Server-side rendering optimization
- Type-safe locale management

**Build Configuration** (`next.config.ts`)
- Extends Next.js base configuration
- Supports static generation for improved performance

**Sound Management** (`src/components/useSounds.ts`)
- Audio caching for performance optimization
- User interaction detection for autoplay compliance
- Cross-component sound sharing

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework**: Next.js 16.2.9 with App Router
**Animation**: Framer Motion 12.40.0 for smooth transitions
**3D Graphics**: @react-three/fiber 9.6.1 and Three.js 0.184.0
**UI Components**: Tailwind CSS 4 for styling
**Internationalization**: @formatjs/intl-localematcher 0.8.10

### Integration Points

**Audio Integration**: Canvas-confetti for celebratory effects
**3D Rendering**: React Three Fiber for WebGL animations
**Performance**: Dynamic imports for selective component loading

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant CF as CreatorFlow
    participant AP as ApologyExperience
    participant S as SoundSystem
    participant T as ThreeJS
    
    U->>CF: Start Creation
    CF->>CF: Collect Information
    CF->>AP: Generate Link
    U->>AP: View Experience
    AP->>T: Initialize 3D Scene
    AP->>S: Play Background Music
    U->>AP: Interact with Elements
    AP->>S: Trigger Sound Effects
    AP->>T: Animate 3D Elements
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Interactive Apology Creation**
- Multi-language support (11 languages including Arabic, Russian, Spanish)
- Scenario-based apology templates
- Personalized recipient names and messages
- Dynamic link generation for easy sharing

**Immersive Experience**
- 3D animated heart with realistic physics
- Particle systems and lighting effects
- Responsive sound design with background music
- Confetti celebration system for successful outcomes

**Gamified Interaction**
- Physics-based "runaway" button that becomes increasingly difficult to click
- Animated progress indicators with sound feedback
- Visual escalation as user attempts to refuse forgiveness
- Celebration animations upon successful apology

### Implementation Highlights

**Dynamic Component Architecture** (`src/components/`)
- Modular component design with clear responsibilities
- Reusable internationalization patterns
- Performance-optimized rendering strategies
- Cross-browser compatibility considerations

**Advanced Animation System**
- Framer Motion integration for complex animations
- Three.js scene management for 3D graphics
- Canvas-based particle effects for visual impact
- Optimized animation loops for smooth performance

**Internationalization Strategy**
- Dynamic dictionary loading based on URL locale
- Right-to-left language support for Arabic
- SEO-friendly metadata generation per locale
- Structured data for improved search visibility

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> CreatorMode
    CreatorMode --> ApologyExperience: Generate Link
    CreatorMode --> CreatorMode: Edit Details
    
    ApologyExperience --> ButtonInteraction: User Input
    ButtonInteraction --> ButtonInteraction: Move Button
    ButtonInteraction --> Success: Click Yes
    ButtonInteraction --> Failure: Click No
    
    Success --> Celebration: Confetti Effects
    Celebration --> [*]
    Failure --> ButtonInteraction: Try Again
```

**Performance Optimization**
- Lazy loading for 3D components to reduce initial bundle size
- Audio caching to prevent redundant downloads
- Efficient animation rendering with proper cleanup
- Progressive enhancement for accessibility

**Cross-Platform Compatibility**
- Mobile-responsive design with touch optimizations
- VoiceOver and screen reader support
- Keyboard navigation for accessibility
- Touch gesture support for mobile devices

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [src/app/layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx](src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx)
- [src/app/[lang]/page.tsx](src/app/[lang]/page.tsx)
- [src/components/ApologyExperience.tsx](src/components/ApologyExperience.tsx)
- [src/components/CreatorFlow.tsx](src/components/CreatorFlow.tsx)
- [src/components/LandingContent.tsx](src/components/LandingContent.tsx)
- [src/app/[lang]/dictionaries.ts](src/app/[lang]/dictionaries.ts)
- [src/components/useSounds.ts](src/components/useSounds.ts)
- [src/components/RunawayButtonI18n.tsx](src/components/RunawayButtonI18n.tsx)
- [src/components/SorryMeterI18n.tsx](src/components/SorryMeterI18n.tsx)
- [src/components/Heart3D.tsx](src/components/Heart3D.tsx)
- [next.config.ts](next.config.ts)

</blog>
